directive.title: Mobile Responsive — Touch Gesture & Navigation UX Polish

auditor.summary: The build successfully implements all mobile navigation UX polish requirements: swipe gestures with passive touch listeners, fixed bottom navigation with safe-area support, sticky column headers, and native mobile picker enforcement. All prior CRITICAL and HIGH findings are resolved. The code is well-structured, uses SVG icons instead of emojis, and properly scopes CSS to mobile breakpoints. Two MEDIUM issues remain: an arbitrary time constraint in the swipe hook that could reject valid slow swipes, and unnecessary JS-based media query detection in BottomNav that adds bundle overhead. These are minor and do not block convergence. The build satisfies all acceptance criteria and is ready to ship.

top_issues.description: The hook uses Date.now() for time tracking with a 50-500ms window. While improved from the previous 300ms cap, this still imposes an arbitrary time constraint not specified in the requirements. The spec says 'within one animation frame' which refers to the response time of the UI update, not the swipe duration. Very slow deliberate swipes (>500ms) on older devices could be incorrectly rejected.

The BottomNav uses a JavaScript-based media query (useState/useEffect with window.innerWidth) instead of Tailwind's responsive classes. This adds client-side JS overhead and a potential flash of content on initial render before the effect runs. The spec requires 'no new JS bundle overhead > 5KB' and this approach adds unnecessary JS.

The main content padding uses 'pb-[calc(56px+env(safe-area-inset-bottom))]' which is a Tailwind arbitrary value. This is valid but the 'bottom-nav-safe' utility class defined in globals.css could be used instead for consistency.
